supply_chain.dependencies: A small number of companies, located primarily in Asia, co-manufacture the vast majority of our brewers. The majority of the distribution of our brewers, beverage concentrates and syrups is handled by third-party order fulfillment companies in the U.S.
supply_chain.risks: Disruption of manufacturing and distribution operations or supply chain, including increased commodity, raw material, packaging, energy, transportation costs. Supply chain constraints due to supplier capacity constraints, labor shortages, port congestion, cybersecurity incidents. Dependence on limited number of suppliers and manufacturers for brewers. Raw materials available from limited number of suppliers or sole supplier. Certain raw materials sourced from countries experiencing civil unrest, political instability, or unfavorable economic conditions.

regulatory_risks: Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act compliance
Federal Trade Commission Act compliance
Robinson-Patman Act compliance
Clayton Act compliance
Sherman Act compliance
Lanham Act compliance
State consumer protection laws
State warning and labeling laws (California's Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act of 1986 - Proposition 65)
Eco-taxes, extended producer responsibility laws, deposit or reuse/refill mandates
Fees on certain products or packaging
Restrictions or bans on use of certain types of packaging, including single-use plastics
Regulations on PFAS and other chemicals of concern
Bans or restrictions on use of certain ingredients or substances in products
Product labeling or warning requirements
Limitations on marketing or sale of products
Taxes on distribution of sugar-sweetened and diet beverages
Environmental, health, and safety laws and regulations
Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act of 1980 (Superfund law)
Income tax laws and regulations in U.S. and foreign jurisdictions
Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 (IRA) - 1% excise tax on net share repurchases
New or increased tariffs, sanctions, quotas, or trade barriers
Import/export restrictions
Anti-corruption and export laws and regulations (U.S. laws applying to international business)
Privacy, data protection, and data security laws (various jurisdictions)
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
Climate change legislation and disclosure obligations

sanction_risks: Restrictions on business activities due to political and economic instability
Deterioration of economic relations between countries
Changes in or terminations of existing trade agreements
Imposition of tariffs (including recent U.S. tariffs imposed or threatened on Canada, Mexico, China, and other countries)
Retaliatory actions by countries subject to tariffs
Impact of ongoing conflicts in Russia, Ukraine, and Middle East on global economy
